meta.title: Earth, the Snake, and the Garden

messages.text: Earth was not one small planet but a massive world with deserts, mountains, oceans, cities, and more adults than he could imagine.
Yet the little prince landed in a silent desert. The first creature he met was a snake, thin as a golden bracelet.

messages.name: Snake
messages.text: Where are the people? It is lonely among men too.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: I fell from the sky. I am looking for friends and perhaps a way back to my flower.

messages.name: Snake
messages.text: My poison sends people back to the earth from which they came. If you ever need help, I can help.
The prince did not understand the offer, but the snake spoke with such quiet power that the desert seemed less empty and more dangerous.
He crossed sand, climbed a mountain, and called into the air. Only an echo returned his words.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: Be my friends. I am alone.

messages.name: Echo
messages.text: I am alone.
On his planet, a mountain would have been a volcano and an answer would have been a rose. Here even his voice came back lonely.
At last he found a road and followed it to a garden filled with roses. Thousands of roses moved in the wind.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: Good morning.

messages.name: Rose
messages.text: Good morning.
They looked like his flower, delicate and proud, yet none recognized him. His rose had said she was unique in the universe.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: I thought I possessed a flower unlike any other. Now I see a whole garden of them.
He lay in the grass and cried. If his rose was only one temporary rose among thousands, what made his leaving matter?
The question hurt more than the snake, more than the desert, more than the echo from the distant mountain.
He had come to explore Earth, but Earth first showed him how large loneliness could become when love lost its shape.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: I am not a great prince. I own a tiny planet, three volcanoes, and one ordinary rose.
He did not know yet that a garden can hide the meaning of one flower until someone teaches the difference.
Later he would learn that Earth held crowds, but his first lesson was emptiness. A massive planet can still meet a traveler with silence.

messages.name: Snake
messages.text: You are pure enough to make me pity you. I am more powerful than the finger of a king.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: Power that hides in poison is strange. I would rather find a road.
The mountain gave him only echo, a poor imitation of company. He began to miss the rose precisely because she never merely repeated him.
When the garden appeared, beauty multiplied until it became painful. Thousands of roses made one rose seem less grand and more necessary.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: If she is not unique by appearance, perhaps I have misunderstood what unique means.
He did not yet have the answer, but the question had changed. The garden had opened a wound that only friendship could explain.
The snake did not chase him. That made the poison more frightening. It waited with patient certainty, like a door that opens only once.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: I came looking for people, but the first voice on Earth offers an ending.
The garden then offered the opposite danger: not death, but too much beauty. It threatened to make his one beloved flower disappear into numbers.
Among the roses, he began to understand that numbers can wound as deeply as they can impress. One beloved thing had been buried under thousands.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: Maybe I was wrong to ask whether she was rare. I should ask whether I was faithful.
That question prepared him for the fox before he knew the fox existed.
